source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
As shown in the figure, a wooden board with mass $M=1\mathrm{kg}$ is at rest on a rough horizontal ground. The coefficient of kinetic friction between the board and the ground is $\mu_{1}=0.1$. A small iron block with mass $m=1\mathrm{kg}$ and negligible size is placed at the left end of the board. The coefficient of kinetic friction between the iron block and the board is $\mu_{2}=0.4$. Take $g=10\mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}^{2}$.

1. If a pulling force of magnitude $F=1\mathrm{N}$ is applied to the right end of the iron block, determine the magnitude of the frictional force acting on the iron block.
2. If a pulling force of magnitude $F=5\mathrm{N}$ is applied to the right end of the iron block, determine the magnitude of the frictional force acting on the iron block.
3. If the length of the wooden board is $L=1\mathrm{m}$, and a constant horizontal force $F=8\mathrm{N}$ is applied to the iron block to the right, how long will it take for the iron block to move to the right end of the board?

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'As shown in the figure'
requires_image=true and text_dominant=false in the input
the setup uses spatial relations (iron block at the left end of the board, moving to the right end)
however, the text itself already provides the key masses, coefficients, force values, and board length, so the image may be supportive rather than strictly necessary
multi_step: the problem has three sub-questions with different force regimes (F=1 N, 5 N, 8 N)
solving requires combining friction laws with force/acceleration reasoning rather than reading off a single fact
part 3 additionally requires relative motion reasoning over board length L=1 m and a time calculation
the final answer contains three separate numeric results, indicating a multi-stage solution structure
verifiability: answer_type is short_text and the provided answer consists of three explicit numeric results with units
all needed physical parameters are specified in the text (masses, friction coefficients, g, force values, board length)
the solution can be checked by standard mechanics equations and case analysis for friction/sliding
asset_registry_record shows registry_passed=true, question_present=true, and answer_present=true

decision_reasons: asset registry passed with no reported issues
question, answer, and primary image are all present, so core semantics are well supported
initial scoring is strong: multi_step_score=0.91 and verifiability_score=0.95
the answer is explicit short text with numeric results and units, which supports verification
the noted risks are limited and do not indicate missing core assets
